notes:
All Platforms:
- The capture hotkey is now three separate actions: Capture All In One (the full picker overlay), Capture Active Window, and Capture Active Monitor. The two new ones capture instantly, with no picker. All default to unbound; set them in Settings > Keyboard Shortcuts.
- Immediate captures follow your delivery settings exactly (copy to clipboard / open the preview editor) and open the editor on the correct monitor.
- The settings window and preview editor now have proper titles ("Cosmic Capture Kit - Settings" / "Cosmic Capture Kit - Preview Editor"). If you float them in a tiling window manager by title, see the updated README rules; COSMIC float exceptions migrate automatically.
- The settings window remembers its size between launches.
- General settings: "Keep system tray icon" (renamed), with a new "Automatically start on login" toggle beneath it.
- When recording system audio with a mic, ducking responds only to real speech from an enabled mic, never to background noise and never when the mic is off.

macOS:
- Keyboard shortcut fields show native modifier symbols (⌃⌥⇧⌘) universally.
- Capturing a full-screen app window now grabs the window contents instead of the capture loading cover.

Windows:
- Windows now live-resizes: the settings window and preview editor re-layout and rescale during a resize drag, matching macOS.
- Recording system audio keeps the dialog (center) channel on surround and spatial endpoints, and loud surround is smoothly limited instead of clipped.
- A recording that hits an encoder stall now recovers and saves instead of hanging forever.
- Single-window captures show the fullscreen loading cover with a cancel button, like macOS.
- Capture selectors no longer appear in the taskbar; only the editor and settings do.
- The system tray is on by default (with start-on-login); both can be switched off in General settings.
- Fixed a crash that could kill the settings window as it opened, and Windows-key chords now display as "Win+...".
